content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch against b171754e8a490bb73f29419fb47f323217b502a4 and found 1 fix-now correctness issue in the new final-review retry path. I also ran targeted `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` checks for the new helper/tests; they pass, but they currently miss the transient-domain-validation case above.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2437-2445 makes every `FailureClass::DomainValidationFailure` terminal before the new transient classifier is consulted. That breaks the exact codex path this bead was meant to fix: `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:2679-2705` explicitly treats `ERROR: stream disconnected before completion` / `You can retry your request...` carried as `DomainValidationFailure` as transient, but the final-review loop will still fail them immediately instead of retrying. The new no-retry test at src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:4494-4587 also hard-codes the wrong rule by asserting that all domain-validation failures are non-retryable, so a 1-reviewer panel can still fail on the reproduced codex disconnect path.
   Rationale: Only bypass retries when the failure is domain-validation and both `is_timeout_related(error)` and `is_transient_codex_failure(error)` are false. Add a regression that scripts `PlannedInvocationFailure::DomainValidation("ERROR: stream disconnected before completion")` and expects a successful retry.
